title: BrowserForwarder 未生效
author: shynome
body:
在提出问题前请先自行排除服务器端问题，同时也请通过搜索确认是否有人提出过相同问题。

找到最相关的是 https://github.com/2dust/v2rayNG/pull/5579 , 但看起来也不是我这个问题

### 预期行为

BrowserForwarder 生效, 在未用QQ浏览器打开专属页面时, 连接应该失败 (我使用了分应用代理, QQ浏览器不代理)

### 实际行为

BrowserForwarder 未生效, 看起来是直接忽略了 `useBrowserForwarding` 选项

### 复现方法

参考此处配置 https://guide.v2fly.org/advanced/wss_and_browserforwarder.html

1. 导出任意完整配置后再导入
2. 为其添加 `browserForwarder` 和 `useBrowserForwarding` 配置
3. 启动并测试连接, 此时应该失败, 但是看到的却是成功并返回了延时
4. 打开浏览器也是正常使用, 感觉上完全没有使用 BrowserForwarder 配置

### 日志信息
<details>

通过`adb logcat -s com.v2ray.ang GoLog V2rayConfigUtilGoLog Main`获取日志。请自行删减日志中可能出现的敏感信息。

如果问题可重现，建议先执行`adb logcat -c`清空系统日志再执行上述命令，再操作重现问题。
```
在这里粘贴日志
```
</details>

### 环境信息

V2rayNG F-Droid v2.2.6

### 额外信息（可选）

测试大王卡QQ浏览器免流

title: (Cloudflare) config stops working in v2rayNG versions after 2.0.18
author: HappyBDayToYou
body:
Before submitting this issue, I checked that the server side is working correctly.

### Expected behavior
The VLESS config created with Cloudflare should connect and work normally.

This same config works correctly in:

- v2rayNG 2.0.18
- the latest v2rayN

### Actual behavior
In versions after v2rayNG 2.0.18, this config no longer works properly.

in test I get: Fail to detect internet connection: io: read/write on closed pipe

### Reproduction method
1.  Import the following VLESS config into v2rayNG
2. Connect to the config
3. run the built-in delay test
4. The connection does not work properly / delay test fails

Thanks for considering this!

title: WireGaurd -1 real delay test
author: mrk1379
body:
Expected Behavior:

When running "Real delay" test on a WireGuard/Warp outbound config, the app should perform the actual real-delay test (an HTTP request through the established tunnel) and report an accurate latency, the same way it did before the TCPing pre-check was added ahead of the real delay test.

Actual Behavior

Since the update that added a TCPing pre-check before the real delay test (v2.5), **all** WireGuard outbound configs (including standard Cloudflare Warp configs) immediately fail with a delay of **-1**, even though the same configs connect and work perfectly fine when actually selected and connected to.
 And do return real delay on older versions. 
 
The root cause appears to be that the new TCPing pre-check performs a raw TCP `connect()` directly to the outbound's `endpoint` IP:port. Wiregaurd do not listen for TCP connections at all. So the TCP pre-check will always time out and return failure, causing the app to skip the real delay test entirely and report -1, regardless of whether the WireGuard tunnel itself is reachable and functional over UDP.

This makes the "Real delay" test (and any subscription/group sorting that relies on it) completely useless for WireGuard-based configs.

Steps to Reproduce

1. Import any working WireGuard (like Cloudflare Warp with wcfg) config into v2rayNG 2.5+ (see example config below).
2. Tap "Real delay" / "Test real delay" on the config or run a group/subscription real-delay test.
3. Observe the result shows **-1** for the config.
4. Manually tap "Connect" on the same config instead — it connects successfully and internet traffic flows normally through it, proving the config is valid and the tunnel works.
5. 
Logs

<details>

```
07-07 13:31:35.699 E/com.v2ray.ang.fdroid(23278): socketConnectTime IOException: failed to connect to /188.114.96.188 (port 8886) from /22.83.225.144 (port 41438) after 1000ms.
```

</details>

Environment

- App: v2rayNG (F-Droid build)
- Version: 2.2.5 -2.2.6
- Device: Android 
- Config type: WireGuard (Cloudflare Warp)

Additional Info

Example config (keys are placeholders/redacted):

```json
{
  "outbounds": [
    {
      "protocol": "wireguard",
      "settings": {
        "address": ["172.16.0.2/32"],
        "mtu": 1280,
        "peers": [
          {
            "endpoint": "188.114.98.138:7103",
            "publicKey": "REDACTED_PEER_PUBLIC_KEY"
          }
        ],
        "reserved": [0, 0, 0],
        "secretKey": "REDACTED_PRIVATE_KEY"
      },
      "tag": "proxy"
    }
  ]
}
```

title: Invalidate Observatory data after network change
author: eliotcougar
body:
I have a set of profiles with different outbounds and different protocols. On WiFi, some outbounds and some protocols don't work, On a mobile connection, another set of outbounds and protocols don't work. The two conditions are very different.
I have a "LeastLoad" or "LeastPing" Policy Group set up across the connections and hope that it will choose whatever is accessible at current time.
When the phone switches between WiFi and cellular, it takes a really long time for the connection to be usable again, because it keeps using the route that was accessible on one network, but became inaccessible on the other network. I often have to Restart Service for it to start working again.

**Solution: Observatory data should be invalidated when the network changes, and the new probe should be initiated immediately.**

_Bonus points for implementing advanced BurstObservatory settings in the PolicyGroup setup: "destination", "connectivity", "interval", "sampling", "timeout", "httpMethod"._

title: Universal APK for v2.2.6 release
author: clockedzero
body:
Hello,

Thank you for your continued work on v2rayNG.

I noticed that the **Universal APK** is not included in the **v2.2.6** release. Only the architecture-specific APKs are available.

Could you please add the Universal APK for this release as well? It is very useful for users who are unsure about their device's CPU architecture or prefer a single APK that works across different devices.

Thank you for your time and your great work on this project.

title: Bug: Failed to open geosite.dat (no such file or directory) on Android 16
author: aligg-rgb
body: 在提出问题前请先自行排除服务器端问题，同时也请通过搜索确认是否有人提出过相同问题。 预期行为 描述你认为应该发生什么 The application should successfully connect to the added configuration without throwing any geodata errors, behaving exactly as it did before the Android 16 update. 实际行为 描述实际发生了什么 When tapping the connect button, a red error message pops up indicating a failure to build the routing configuration. Specifically, it fails to open geosite.dat because it cannot find the file in the app's external data directory (/storage/emulated/0/Android/data/com.v2ray.ang.fdroid/files/assets/geosite.dat). This appears to be a storage path or permission issue triggered by the Android 16 update, preventing the app from accessing its own asset files. 复现方法 Update the device to Android 16 (Security Patch: June 1, 2026). Open the v2rayNG app and add a valid configuration. Tap the connect button. 日志信息 通过adb logcat -s com.v2ray.ang GoLog V2rayConfigUtilGoLog Main获取日志。请自行删减日志中可能出现的敏感信息。 如果问题可重现，建议先执行adb logcat -c清空系统日志再执行上述命令，再操作重现问题。 config error: infra/conf/serial: failed to parse json config > infra/conf: failed to build routing configuration > infra/conf: invalid field rule > common/geodata: illegal domain rule: geosite:google > common/geodata: failed to open geosite.dat > stat /storage/emulated/0/Android/data/com.v2ray.ang.fdroid/files/assets/geosite.dat: no such file or directory 环境信息 OS Version: Android 16 Security Patch: June 1, 2026 App Package/Version: Tested on multiple versions, specifically using the F-Droid build (com.v2ray.ang.fdroid) 额外信息（可选） I have tested another app called V2RAYGG from Google Play (which is very similar to v2rayNG but uses an older Xray core), and it connects without any issues on this exact same device. I also tested the MAHSANG app, and it works perfectly fine. I have tried downloading and installing different versions of v2rayNG, but the "no such file or directory" error for geosite.dat persists across all of them. This confirms the issue is specifically isolated to how the newer v2rayNG versions or Xray cores handle file paths and scoped storage in Android 16.

title: Missing texts from localization file
author: Blueberryy
body: Here i collected screenshots of stuff that i could not find on translation file of v2rayNG; Notification channel names for app settings Open source licenses window title and possibly whole page File sizes On App selection page there supposed to be 3 buttons, but if translation text for first 2 is too long, the 3rd one will be very thin **Default** group name **v** for app version **ms** for profile latency And also, since Privacy Policy is in Chinese, i think it would be good to change button text and add info that it's not in English like just add suffix to it like this: Privacy Policy (In Chinese) https://github.com/2dust/v2rayNG/blob/f226ace30dd86aafff0a922854da02621d66f28f/V2rayNG/app/src/main/res/values/strings.xml#L270 hope that those gotta be added in next updates, thanks!

title: Feature Request: Add UDP connectivity testing (like v2rayN Windows) / 功能请求：增加 UDP 连通性测试（类似 Windows 版 v2rayN）
author: montiego
body:
🇬🇧 English

Feature Request: Add UDP connectivity testing (like v2rayN Windows)

Please add a feature to v2rayNG that allows testing UDP connectivity for configurations, similar to the v2rayN Windows client.

Currently, v2rayNG only provides TCP-based or general latency tests, but there is no way to verify whether UDP traffic works properly for a selected configuration.

It would be very useful to have a UDP test function, such as:

UDP ping / handshake test
UDP packet reachability check
Or a simple UDP echo test through the proxy
Indication whether UDP is supported for the current config

This is especially important for:

WireGuard / WARP chains
QUIC / HTTP3 traffic
Gaming and real-time applications
DNS over UDP testing

v2rayN on Windows already includes similar functionality, and adding it to v2rayNG would greatly improve debugging and reliability for mobile users.

Thank you 🙏

🇨🇳 中文

功能请求：增加 UDP 连通性测试（类似 Windows 版 v2rayN）

希望 v2rayNG 能增加一个用于测试配置 UDP 连通性的功能，类似 Windows 版 v2rayN 的 UDP 测试功能。

目前 v2rayNG 只有 TCP 延迟测试或基础连通性测试，但无法确认某个配置是否真正支持 UDP 传输。

建议增加以下 UDP 测试能力：

UDP ping / 握手测试
UDP 数据包可达性检测
通过代理进行简单 UDP echo 测试
显示当前配置是否支持 UDP

该功能对于以下场景非常重要：

WireGuard / WARP 链式代理
QUIC / HTTP3 协议
游戏和实时通信
DNS UDP 测试

Windows 版 v2rayN 已经支持类似功能，如果 v2rayNG 也加入，将极大提升移动端调试能力和稳定性。

谢谢 🙏

title: Feature Request: Add Built-in Speed Test for Servers
author: alinasrollahzadeh405-sudo
body:
Hi, thanks for the great work on V2RayNG. 

I would like to suggest a new feature: **Built-in Speed Test**.

**Why is this needed?**
Currently, users have to manually check their connection speed or use external apps to see if a server is performing well. Having a native speed test feature within the app would allow users to:
1. Quickly identify the best/fastest server in their list.
2. Troubleshoot connection issues more efficiently.
3. Improve the overall user experience by reducing the need to switch between apps.

**Suggested Implementation:**
* A simple "Speed Test" button for each server or a bulk test for the whole list.
* A basic ping/latency test followed by a short download/upload check.

I believe this feature would be a massive upgrade for the app. Thanks for considering this request!
